cover.title: Audit report
cover.sanitized_banner: 🔒 **SANITIZED MODE** — no cell values in this report. Formulas, structure, smells, and VBA source are preserved; every non-formula cell value has been replaced with `<redacted>`.
cover.headline: Headline: complexity {complexity}, {pillar_count} pillar cell(s), {smell_count} smell finding(s).
cover.subline_1: Tier 1 audit. Pure static analysis — no AI, no Excel, no macro execution.
cover.subline_2: Same input always produces the same output. Findings ranked, not interpreted.
exec_summary.heading: Executive Summary
exec_summary.most_referenced_group: **Most-referenced cell group**: `{location}` ({member_count} cells, fan-in {fan_in}). Each cell drives {fan_in} calculations.
exec_summary.single_most_impactful: **Single most-impactful cell**: `{location}`{value_phrase} feeds {fan_in} formulas across {affected_sheet_count} sheet(s). Change it, change them all.
exec_summary.top_anomaly: **Top data-anomaly**: in a cluster of {cluster_size} similar formulas, value `{outlier_value}` deviates from the norm `{mode_value}` at `{outlier_location}` (confidence: {confidence}).
exec_summary.entry_points: **Operational entry points**: {n_buttons} button(s), {n_events} event handler(s). See Workflow Guide for the walkthrough.
exec_summary.no_entry_points: **No buttons or event handlers detected** — workbook is formula-driven only. Users interact via cell entries directly.
exec_summary.unknown_domain: **Detected domain**: _unknown_ — domain not auto-detected; analyze manually.
exec_summary.headline_pillar_group: **Pillar group**: `{location}` ({member_count} cells) each cascade into {fan_in} formulas.
exec_summary.headline_anomaly: **Anomaly**: position #{position} of a {cluster_size}-cell cluster normally `{mode_value}` but `{outlier_value}` at `{outlier_location}` ({confidence} conf).
workflow.heading: Workflow Guide
workflow.intro: Inferred from the workbook's VBA structure and embedded form-control buttons (no AI). This is how the workbook is operationally used.
workflow.what_this_means: **What this means**: the steps below describe the sequence a user follows — typically: open the workbook, enter inputs, click button(s), read the resulting cells. This is structural inference; semantic narrative (e.g. "this calculates capacity utilization") would require Track B LLM augmentation.
workflow.no_buttons: No user-callable buttons or event handlers detected — this workbook appears to be formula-driven only (no macro entry points found in `xl/drawings/*` or VBA event subs). Users would interact with the workbook by editing input cells and reading formula outputs directly.
workflow.step_line: **Step {order}**: User clicks button **{label}** on sheet `{sheet}` (bound to `{module_name}.{sub_name}`).
data_flow.heading: Data Flow Story
data_flow.intro: Plain-language description of how data flows between sheets, before the schematic diagram. Derived from formula cross-sheet references and VBA write targets.
data_flow.what_this_means: **What this means**: each paragraph below tells you whether a sheet is **input** (user types here), **derived** (populated by formulas or macros), or **mixed** — and where its values come from / go to.
data_flow.role_input: **input sheet** — no formulas, no inbound cross-sheet references, no VBA writes. Likely user-driven manual entry.
data_flow.role_derived_no_formulas: **derived sheet (no formulas)** — populated by VBA macros or referenced as a source by other sheets but contains only values.
data_flow.role_aggregator: **aggregator/output sheet** — many of its formulas pull from other sheets; downstream usage limited.
data_flow.role_computed: **computed sheet** — populated by formulas with cross-sheet lookups.
data_flow.pillar_count_line: **Pillar cells**: {count} cell(s) in this sheet are pillars (high fan-in change points).
data_flow.manual_override_line: **Manual override risk**: cell `{ref}` has cached error `{error_token}` — see Risk indicators §8.6.
data_flow.diagram_intro: Cross-sheet formula references. Edge label = number of formulas with cross-sheet refs from source -> target sheet. Yellow = hidden, red = veryHidden.
pillar.heading: Pillar cells — systemic single-points-of-impact
pillar.intro: Cells with the highest fan-in (most-referenced). Modifying any of these cascades through many formulas; treat them as critical change points. Equivalent column cells (same fan-in, same affected sheets) are grouped.
pillar.what_this_means: **What this means**: each row is a cell whose value flows into many formulas — change it once, change many calculations at once. The Value and Label columns answer Michael's #1 ask: "what IS this cell?".
pillar.none_at_threshold: No cells reach the pillar threshold (fan-in ≥ {threshold}).
anomaly.heading: Magic-number anomalies — outliers within formula clusters
anomaly.intro: Inside groups of cells that share the same formula shape, this section flags positions where a small minority uses a different numeric constant. These are exactly the kind of "why is this row's discount different?" findings that often indicate a missed update or a deliberate (but undocumented) carve-out.
anomaly.none_detected: No magic-number anomalies detected. Either no large duplicated-formula clusters were found, or every cluster's numeric constants are perfectly consistent.
smells.heading: Smells catalog (Hermans 2015)
smells.more_findings: ({n} more findings of this type — see `audit.json`.)
magic_index.heading: Magic-number index (top 20)
vba.heading: VBA modules + classification
vba.walkthrough_intro: Per-module heuristic narrative, ordered by call-graph dependency from user-callable entry points (button-bound + event handlers). **This is structural narration, not semantic** — we report what reads/writes and what calls what, but not what the code MEANS for the business (that's Track B / LLM augmentation).
vba.walkthrough_what_this_means: **What this means**: each module gets a 4-line summary: structural role, what it does (sheets it reads/writes, modules it calls), notable patterns (error handling, magic numbers, loops), and call relationships.
vba.no_entry_caveat: No buttons or event handlers detected, so no call-graph entry point — modules below are ranked by LOC (a structural proxy for likely importance). Each module is flagged as 'possibly dead code' since static analysis can't confirm reachability without an entry point.
vba.many_dead_caveat: **Caveat for many-dead-modules workbooks**: when a large fraction of modules are flagged 'possibly dead', the VBA may be inherited from another project (donor code), left from prior workbook iterations, or genuinely orphaned. Static analysis cannot disambiguate — Track B (LLM-augmented) reads the code to tell. For now, treat 'possibly dead' as 'no entry point detected', not 'definitely unused'.
vba.more_reachable: ({n} more reachable module(s) — see Reference Appendix §8.7 VBA modules table for full list.)
vba.dead_code_intro: The following modules are not reached from any detected button or event handler. They may be legacy code, helper libraries pulled in but unused, or detection misses (ActiveX controls, dynamic VBA calls). Audit before deleting.
vba.diagram_overview_intro: Modules grouped under their inferred type.
vba.details_heading: VBA module details — external keywords & range literals:
diagrams.sheet_dataflow_intro: Cross-sheet formula references. Edge label = number of formulas with cross-sheet refs from source -> target sheet. Yellow = hidden, red = veryHidden, default = visible.
diagrams.pillar_impact_intro: Top-{top_n} pillar cells and the sheets they cascade into.
diagrams.no_pillars_node: No pillar cells reach the threshold
diagrams.no_buttons_node: No buttons or event handlers detected
top_impact.heading: Top Impact Findings
top_impact.intro: Top-N filtered list across pillars, anomalies, smells, and risks. Full catalogs live in the Reference Appendix (§8).
top_impact.pillars_see_full: See §8.1 for the full pillar table; each row's narrative explains the cell's role and impact.
top_impact.no_anomalies: No magic-number anomalies detected. Either no large duplicated-formula clusters, or every cluster's numbers are perfectly consistent.
top_impact.smells_what_this_means: **What this means**: code-smell categories from Hermans 2015. Not bugs — patterns that often indicate maintainability risk.
top_impact.risk_very_hidden: **{n} veryHidden sheet(s)**: `{names}` — invisible in Excel UI even via Hide/Unhide menu; only VBA reveals them.
top_impact.risk_hidden: **{n} hidden sheet(s)**: `{names}` — not visible by default but can be unhidden via right-click.
top_impact.risk_errors: **{n} cell(s) with cached formula errors** (e.g. #REF!, #N/A) — see §8.6.
top_impact.risk_external: **{n} external workbook reference pattern(s)** — fragile cross-file links.
domain_findings.heading: Domain-Specific Findings
domain_findings.intro: Detected domain templates (e.g. manufacturing/capacity-planning, logistics/routing). For each, we cross-checked the workbook against industry-known hardcoded-risk constants and scheduling-method hallmarks.
domain_findings.what_this_means: **What this means**: domain templates pre-populate "things to look for" specific to a vertical. They're a heuristic checklist, not a verdict — every hit deserves a closer look but isn't necessarily a bug.
domain_findings.expected_roles_found: **Expected sheet roles found in this workbook**:
domain_findings.role_missing: `{role}` — no sheet name matches; confirm whether absent or named differently
domain_findings.hardcode_risks_none: **Common hardcode risks**: no clear matches found in pillars / magic-number index. Either the risks aren't present or are named differently.
appendix.heading: Reference Appendix
appendix.intro: Full data tables and indices. Every catalog the audit produces lives below — for technical readers verifying findings or following up on a Top Impact entry.
appendix.pillars_intro: Full deduped pillar list — see Top Impact §5 for the Top-5 view.
appendix.pillars_none_at_threshold: No cells reach the pillar threshold.
named_ranges.heading: Named ranges
sheets.heading: Sheets
complexity.heading: Complexity score
risks.heading: Risk indicators
metadata.heading: File metadata
basic_stats.heading: Basic statistics
glossary.heading: Glossary
glossary.intro: Plain-language definitions for terms used throughout this report. Alphabetical.
glossary.anomaly: anomaly (magic-number)||Inside a cluster of cells sharing the same formula shape, a position where a small minority uses a different numeric constant. Often signals a missed update or undocumented carve-out.
glossary.audit: audit||The full report this tool produces — markdown + JSON + HTML — for one xlsm workbook. Pure static analysis, zero LLM, zero network.
glossary.byoa: BYOA (Bring Your Own AI)||Distribution model where the customer uses their own LLM subscription. We do not call any LLM; we just package context for them to paste into Copilot / Claude / etc.
glossary.column_block: column-block (pillar)||When N cells in the same column share the same fan-in count and the same set of dependent sheets, we collapse them into one pillar entry with `member_count = N` instead of N separate rows.
glossary.complexity_score: complexity score||A 0-100 composite of 5 sub-scores (data scale, formula depth, metadata complexity, smell density, VBA mass). Higher = harder to refactor.
glossary.confidence: confidence (high/medium/low)||How certain we are about a finding. `high` = exact deterministic count; `medium` = tokenizer-based with well-defined rules; `low` = statistical inference or analysis skipped.
glossary.vba_classes: data-loader / transformer / report-writer / ui-handler||VBA module classifications based on Sub/Function naming patterns (`Load*`, `Calc*`, `Print*`, `*_Click`) and structural counts (reads/writes/loops). Heuristic only.
glossary.dead_suspected: dead-suspected (VBA)||A module with no Sub calls, no value writes, no name signals — likely empty or unused. May still be reachable through dynamic VBA calls; verify before deleting.
glossary.domain: domain||An industry vertical we detected via keyword matching: capacity-planning / inventory-supply-chain / logistics-routing / operations-s&op / actuarial-insurance / financial-modeling. Triggers domain-specific sections when matched.
glossary.duplicated_formulas: duplicated-formulas||Cells whose normalized formula pattern appears in many places. Often legitimate (column-fill), but can hide outliers — see Magic-number Anomalies.
glossary.fan_in: fan-in||How many distinct formulas reference a given cell. Higher fan-in = modifying that cell ripples through more calculations.
glossary.formula_relay: formula relay (pillar)||A pillar cell that itself is a formula — others read its result, and it is itself derived. Modifying the formula changes downstream derivations.
glossary.hermans_smells: Hermans smells||Spreadsheet code-smell catalog from Felienne Hermans's 2015 paper (multiple-references, conditional-complexity, multiple-operations, duplicated-formulas, magic-numbers, long-calculation-chain).
glossary.incoming_outgoing: incoming / outgoing (cell)||Cell A's `incoming` set is every cell whose formula reads A. Cell A's `outgoing` set is every cell A's formula reads. Together they form the cell-level dataflow graph.
glossary.llm_augment: LLM-AUGMENT marker||An HTML comment like `<!-- LLM-AUGMENT: vba-narration:Module1 -->` marking a section a future Track B (LLM) ingest step can replace with richer prose. Invisible in rendered Markdown.
glossary.magic_number: magic number||A non-trivial numeric literal (not 0/1/2/10/100/-1) embedded directly in formula or VBA code. Usually a candidate for extraction into a named constant.
glossary.on_error: On Error Resume Next||VBA directive that silently skips the next failing line. Risky pattern: errors are suppressed, code keeps running with potentially-bad state. Always reviewed in the audit.
glossary.pillar: pillar cell||A cell with high fan-in (≥ 20 by default) — modifying it cascades to many formulas. The audit's primary diagnostic for "what should I never accidentally change?"
glossary.sanitize: Sanitize mode||Optional `--sanitize` flag that replaces every non-formula cell value with `<redacted>` before any analysis. Formulas, VBA structure, and counts are preserved. Designed for sharing the audit without leaking the workbook's data.
glossary.smell: smell||A code pattern the audit flags as worth a second look. Not a bug — a heuristic 'smelly' indicator borrowed from software engineering.
glossary.tier: tier (1/1.5/2/3/4/5)||Our product tiers — Tier 1 = this audit (free, zero-LLM); Tier 1.5 = LLM-assisted comprehension (BYOA); Tier 2 = exec risk report; Tier 3 = refactored Python prototype; etc.
glossary.track_a_b: Track A / Track B||Two-track architecture for Tier 1 reports. Track A is fully static (no LLM); Track B is BYOA — tool produces a dossier + mega-prompt the user pastes into their own Copilot / Claude, then pastes the response back.
glossary.very_hidden: veryHidden sheet||Excel hides three states: visible / hidden / veryHidden. veryHidden cannot be unhidden through the right-click menu — only VBA can reveal it. Often used for internal config or audit-trail data the user shouldn't touch.
glossary.workflow_step: workflow step||One operational step a user performs — typically clicking a button or triggering an event handler. Derived from xl/drawings + VBA static analysis, then topologically sorted by sheet write/read dependency.
methodology.heading: Methodology
methodology.smell_thresholds: Smell thresholds: multiple-references ≥ {mr}; long-calculation-chain depth ≥ {lcc}; conditional-complexity nesting ≥ {cc}; multiple-operations ≥ {mo}; duplicated-formulas pattern frequency ≥ {df}.
methodology.logic_depth: Logic-depth thresholds: pillar fan-in ≥ {pfm} (top {ptn} after dedupe); anomaly cluster size ≥ {acms}, outlier fraction ≤ {aof}.
methodology.pillar_dedupe: Pillar dedupe: cells in the same column with identical fan-in and identical affected-sheet set are collapsed into one column-block entry. After dedupe we surface the top {top_n} distinct entries.
methodology.pillar_dedupe_short: Pillar dedupe: cells in the same column with identical fan-in and identical affected-sheet set are collapsed into one column-block entry.
methodology.confidence_semantics: Confidence semantics: `high` = exact / deterministic count; `medium` = tokenizer-based with well-defined rules; `low` = statistical inference or analysis skipped due to scale.
methodology.domain_detector: Domain hint detector: pure keyword matching (case-insensitive, word-boundary) against sheet names, named ranges, and VBA Sub/Function names. No LLM, no inference.
methodology.domain_detector_short: Domain hint detector: pure keyword matching (case-insensitive, word-boundary). No LLM, no inference.
methodology.trivial_numbers: Trivial numbers excluded from magic-number index: {numbers}.
methodology.reliability: Reliability contract: same input → byte-identical `audit.md`, `audit.json`, and `audit.html`. All outputs use sorted dict keys and lexicographic list ordering. No timestamps. No CDN fetch at audit time.
methodology.sanitize_active: **Sanitize mode active**: every non-formula cell value has been replaced with `<redacted>` before any analysis ran. Formulas, VBA source, smells, pillars, anomalies, and structural counts remain accurate. The SHA-256 above is of the original file.
narrative.pillar.group: {member_count} cells in this column block{inline} each cascade into {fan_in} formulas across {scope} — modifying the column header or any single cell propagates similarly ({risk}).
narrative.pillar.single: Modifying `{location}`{inline} (a {kind_word}) would cascade into {fan_in} formulas across {scope} — {risk}.
narrative.pillar.risk_high: treat as a critical change point
narrative.anomaly.text: In a cluster of {cluster_size} formulas matching `{sample_formula}`, position #{position} usually equals `{mode_display}` ({mode_count}/{cluster_size} = {mode_share}%), but {cell_phrase} use `{outlier_display}` (deviation {dev}). Confirm whether this is intentional.
narrative.vba.role_isolated: isolated module — not reached from any button or event handler (possibly dead)
narrative.vba.role_leaf_helper: leaf helper — invoked by other modules but invokes nothing further
narrative.vba.role_entry_point: system entry point — invokes other modules and is not invoked back
narrative.vba.role_large: large multi-purpose module — likely the workbook's main logic block
narrative.vba.role_data_loader: data ingest — reads external sources or workbook ranges into VBA structures
narrative.vba.role_transformer: compute step — reads inputs, derives outputs, writes back to sheets
narrative.vba.role_report_writer: output writer — populates sheet ranges with computed values
narrative.vba.role_ui_handler: UI event handler — fires when the user opens the workbook or interacts with a sheet
narrative.vba.role_dead_shell: near-empty shell — typical for default class/sheet stubs with no real code
narrative.vba.role_mixed: mixed responsibilities — no single structural signal dominates
narrative.vba.no_io: contains no salient sheet I/O or inter-module calls
narrative.vba.oer_with_lines: Contains `On Error Resume Next` at line(s) {lines} — silent failure risk; errors are suppressed silently.
narrative.vba.oer_no_lines: Contains `On Error Resume Next` — silent failure risk.
narrative.vba.nested_loops_pattern: Contains {n} levels of nested loops — typically indicates a row × column × period scan pattern.
narrative.vba.no_callers: not called by any other module
